transcript: So I want to give you a little talk not about BSOL, not about our ETF, although I'd love to do that, but just about why ETFs matter. Y'all know about Bitwise so I'll skip that over. This is what I'm going to talk about: Why ETFs matter, how they work, what to expect, and how we talk to institutions about Solana in eight minutes and 52 seconds. I wanted to start here. Everyone in crypto feels really good about the size of this industry. We're a 3.1 trillion dollar industry from a market cap perspective. The first reason ETFs matter is that actually, we're like a tiny planet in a broader solar system. That's the size of the equity and fixed income markets, massively larger. That's why this question of when the institutions are coming matters so much because even a little bit of capital from this 260 trillion dollar investment market going into our little 3.1 trillion dollar market would have a big effect. And of course, Solana is like a moon on the planet crypto, right, amidst this broader solar system. So it is a really big deal. The second reason ETFs matter is this is a chart of ETF assets. I know this looks like a crypto chart, it's up only, but this is a chart of ETF assets since 2003. They're the fastest growing financial product in the world. ETFs added more AUM last year than the entire size of the crypto market. So ETFs really do matter. This is how the largest institutions work. The reason they do it is because they're easy. I know a lot of people wonder why do institutions use ETFs when they could just own the asset natively. This is a study we do, we ask thousands of investors every year how do they want to invest in crypto. These are the largest institutions, family offices, financial advisors in the world. We've done it for eight years. Every single year, it's ETFs at about a 70% rate. This is just what they want, it's what they know, and so it's how they want to invest. Again, you may wonder why. Financial institutions and financial professionals don't spend all of their time studying the markets. This is from a study that looked at how financial advisors spend their day. They work about 55 hours a week. They spend five hours of that on investment management. A lot of people think of institutions, particularly financial advisors, spending all day studying the markets. They spend 10% of their time studying the markets. Their average allocation to crypto might be 1% of their portfolio. That means each week they spend three minutes studying and thinking about the crypto market. That is actually how the numbers knit out. So why do they like ETFs? Because they know them, they trust them, they make it easy, and in three minutes they can buy it. The last one is that ETFs are just a great tool. This is a fun fact about ETFs, which is not true in any other area of the market, which is that you or my father can literally buy the same ETF as the Harvard Management Company or leading sovereign wealth funds around the world. That's pretty cool. That's true in crypto. We can buy the same Bitcoin, we can buy the same Solana as the largest institutions in the world, but it's not true in most of the investing world where we don't get access to it. So it's easy, it's a high quality product, it solves all of the use cases, etc. All right, how do ETFs work? Someone asked me to do this because not everyone understands how ETFs work. I'll give you the one minute version of a talk I gave a thousand times, which is about 45 minutes long. ETFs are relatively simple. So an ETF like BSOL will trade on the Nasdaq or the New York Stock Exchange at a value. Let's say it holds $25 of Solana for every share. And it should trade at $25. And usually it does. But if a bunch of people want to buy BSOL, it will trade up to $25 and one cent. And there'll be this slight arbitrage. When that happens, institutional market makers create more shares of Solana by sending us cash. What do we do with that cash? We take it and we buy Solana, usually from institutional market makers, we put it in cold storage and we stake it. We sell shares of that ETF to these institutions at $25 and they sell them in the market at $25 and a penny. That brings the price back down to $25 so that the price always equals the amount of Solana that we have in the fund. That's been working in ETF land for 30 years and it works more or less perfectly. All right, what to expect from ETFs going forward? Why will they shape the returns of Solana in part? Why will they contribute to those returns? I thought I would start with this. These are the cumulative inflows into Bitcoin ETFs. Bitcoin's launched in January of 2024. Most people know this story by now. But there have been 9,000 ETF launches in U.S. history. And the number one ETF launch of all time out of all 9,000 was the Bitcoin ETF. And not by a small factor. It pulled in 36 billion dollars in assets in its first year. Number two on the list, which is the Nasdaq 100, many people know the ticker QQQ, number two on the list did 6 billion. So it was the most important ETF launch of all time, not by a little, but by a factor of six, which is pretty phenomenal. We've had steady monthly flows. Not exclusively monthly inflows. Every few months there are monthly outflows, but mostly monthly inflows to the tune of four, five, six billion dollars a year. And this is why it has an impact. If you look since the Bitcoin ETF launched and you look at the amount of Bitcoin that was produced by the network, the network produced 360,000 Bitcoin and ETFs bought 700,000 Bitcoin. I'm a CIO so I do complex math. 700,000 is more than 360,000. And so the price of Bitcoin has doubled since the ETF launched. It can be as simple as that. I don't like to show many Ethereum slides, but it makes the point. I'm getting to Solana. The same thing is true with Ethereum ETFs. Here are the monthly flows, much lower than Bitcoin but still significant. Here is the supply of ETH since Ethereum ETFs launched versus the amount of ETH that ETFs have bought. And the price is up as well. Here is Solana. You can see it's following a similar pattern. You'll see that the chart has sort of two phases. There was an initial Solana ETF which wasn't in a traditional structure. Bitwise launched BSOL on October 28th and you see it's been significantly rising ever since. These are the daily flows. You can see that we're getting something like 40 or 50 million dollars of flows into our Solana ETF on an average day. Not every day. Some days there have been very little flows. There have been no days of outflows, but 30 or 40 million dollars. This next slide didn't turn out. It covers actually that whole period so I want to put it in context. Since those first ETFs launched, before Bitwise came onto the scene, we haven't quite caught up with the amount of new Solana being produced. But if you look since we launched, it's something like three or four X. So right now the Bitwise Solana ETF is buying three or four X the amount of new Solana coming into the market. And more demand than supply tends to lead to higher prices. This is the last slide I'm going to show on ETFs because this is the most important slide. This is actually a slide of Gold ETF inflows. And what I want you to take away from this chart, when the Gold ETFs launched in 2004, they were the second biggest ETF launch of all time, right, sort of the early version of tokenized gold. Everyone was very excited. They did 3 billion dollars of flows in year one. But look what happened in year two, year three, year four, year five, year six, actually out to year 16. The flows continued to go up. And this is what's going to happen in ETF land. I would ask all of you to think, the first time you bought Solana, have you bought any since? Or did you put it all in the very first time you invested? The same thing is happening in ETFs. So the flows that I just showed, where Solana and Bitcoin ETFs are buying more than 100% of the supply, that is going to continue and in fact accelerate over time as we open up these new markets. I have 30 seconds left. I'll just close with this. Bitwise is just one small piece of the puzzle, but a fun thing to say is we do 15,000 meetings with institutional investors every year. And I can tell you that often Solana comes up. We don't have much time to make the case for Solana. As I mentioned, the average person spends three minutes on crypto. But generally speaking, I think there's a very easy case to be made to these institutions. You can say that Solana gives you two bets at once. And this is why it's, I think, the most exciting opportunity in crypto right now. It gives you exposure to the growth of stablecoins, tokenization, DeFi, and other aspects. That's an enormously attractive growth, right? The SEC Chair said the amount of AUM in tokenized stocks could 100,000 X in the next few years. That's an enormous bet. And then you also get this second bet, which is that Solana is going to take an increasing share of that market. If you get both of those bets right, it's not 1 + 1 = 2, it's 2 * 2 = 4 or 3 * 3 = 9. It's exponential growth. And I think that makes Solana the most exciting opportunity in crypto at these current prices. And we love telling institutional investors about it. That's it for me. Thanks everybody.

sources.title: Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
sources.snippet:
Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in AUM
# Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
San Francisco •Nov 21, 2025
![Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management](https://bitwiseinvestments.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.datocms-assets.com%2F62087%2F1762788442-bsol-bitwise-solana-staking-etf-press-500m-aum.png&w=3840&q=75)
Bitwise Asset Management, the global crypto asset manager with over $15 billion in client assets, announced today that the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) has surpassed $500 million in assets under management (AUM), achieving the landmark figure in its first 18 days of trading.
"We are truly in awe of the speed and scale of BSOL’s growth," said Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley. "Crypto is becoming a mainstream asset class. BSOL hitting half a billion dollars in AUM this quickly is a clear testament to investors’ belief in the Solana ecosystem as a cornerstone of the future of the crypto industry."
As one of the first crypto ETPs with built-in staking, BSOL aims to stake 100% of the Fund’s SOL holdings through Bitwise Onchain Solutions, powered by Helius, the leading Solana staking technology known for its high performance, technical excellence, and security. The strategy seeks to maximize investors’ participation in Solana’s average staking rewards of over 7%.¹
BSOL has a net sponsor fee of 0.00% for the first three months on its first $1 billion in assets. All staking fees are also waived for the first three months. After three months, the sponsor fee will be 0.20% and the staking fee, which applies only to the staking rewards that BSOL earns, will be 0.06%.
###### **Note:**
###### (1) Staking reward rate is based on the annualized 90-day average as of 11/20/25, with data from Helius. Rewards are subject to change and should not be considered an indication of the Fund’s performance. The tax treatment of rewards earned by the Trust may have potentially adverse consequences for shareholders. Please consult with your tax advisor.
### Risks and Important Information
**This material must be accompanied by a prospectus. Please read the prospectus carefully before investing. To obtain a current prospectus visit**[**bsoletf.com/welcome**](https://bsoletf.com/welcome)**.**
**The Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) is not suitable for all investors. An investment in BSOL is subject to a high degree of risk, has the potential for significant volatility, and could result in significant or complete loss of investment. BSOL is not an investment company registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended (the “1940 Act”) and therefore is not subject to the same protections as ETFs and mutual funds registered under the 1940 Act. An investment in BSOL is not the same as a direct investment in Solana (SOL).**
Shares of ETPs are bought and sold at market price (not NAV) and are not individually redeemed from the Fund. Brokerage commissions will reduce returns. The NAV may not always correspond to the market price of SOL and, as a result, Creation Units may be created or redeemed at a value that is different from the market price of the Shares. Authorized Participants’ buying and selling activity associated with the creation and redemption of Creation Units may adversely affect an investment in the Shares.
The amount of SOL represented by a Share will continue to be reduced during the life of the Fund due to the transfer of the Fund’s SOL to pay for the Sponsor’s management fee, and to pay for litigation expenses or other extraordinary expenses. This dynamic will occur irrespective of whether the trading price of the Shares rises or falls in response to changes in the price of SOL.
There is no guarantee or assurance that the Fund’s methodology will result in the Fund achieving positive investment returns or outperforming other investment products.
Investors may choose to use the Fund as a means of investing

sources.title: BSOL Options Trading Launches Two Weeks After ETF Debut - Coinspeaker
sources.snippet:
### Key Notes

- Bitwise BSOL options began trading Nov 11 with strike prices from $16-$26 and expirations through May 2026.
- The fund controls $497.2 million in assets and captures 98% of total Solana ETF market inflows since launching.
- BSOL stakes all holdings for 7.20% net rewards with zero fees on first $1 billion through January 2026.

Options trading on the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) began on Nov. 11, adding derivatives to the spot exchange-traded fund that launched on Oct. 28. The development enables institutional investors to hedge positions and implement complex trading strategies on the [Solana](https://www.coinspeaker.com/coins/solana/) [SOL$142.724h volatility:0.4%Market cap:$79.83 BVol. 24h:$4.08 B](https://www.coinspeaker.com/coins/solana/)
exposure vehicle.

Bitwise President Teddy Fusaro and CEO Hunter Horsley confirmed the options launch through X posts on Nov. 11, with Bloomberg terminal screenshots showing active call and put contracts. Coinspeaker confirmed the options are available via Interactive Brokers with strike prices ranging from $16 to $26.

> Milestone today —
>
> Options are now live and trading on [$BSOL](https://twitter.com/search?q=%24BSOL&src=ctag&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw), the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF.
>
> Bridges are opening to investment professionals. [https://t.co/ArkFXFr9ED](https://t.co/ArkFXFr9ED)
>
> — Hunter Horsley (@HHorsley) [November 11, 2025](https://twitter.com/HHorsley/status/1988294519532741006?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)

Options chains display multiple expiration dates including Nov. 21, 2025, Dec. 19, 2025, Feb. 20, 2026, and May 15, 2026. The fund reported 22.4 million shares outstanding as of Nov. 10, [according to SEC filings](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2045872/000119312525274506/0001193125-25-274506-index.htm).

BSOL holds $497.2 million in assets under management with 22.7 million shares outstanding, making it America’s largest Solana ETF, [according to official fund data from Nov. 9](https://bsoletf.com/). Farside Investors only accounts for $329.7 million. However, the fund has indeed captured approximately 98% of total Solana ETF inflows during its initial trading period, outpacing Grayscale’s GSOL.

Coinspeaker Solana ETF Flow Analysis \| Source: Farside

## ETF Structure and Launch

The fund launched on Oct. 28 on NYSE Arca with a 0.20% management fee, waived entirely on the first $1 billion in assets through Jan. 28, 2026, [according to Bitwise’s official announcement](https://bitwiseinvestments.com/newsroom/the-bitwise-solana-staking-etf-bsol-begins-trading). BSOL stakes 100% of its Solana holdings to generate a 7.20% net staking reward rate, with rewards automatically reinvested into the fund. The Trust operates as a grantor trust for federal tax purposes, meaning income and expenses flow through to shareholders.

Authorized Participants create or redeem shares in blocks of 10,000 units at net asset value, either through in-kind Solana transfers or cash settlements. Grayscale’s GSOL represents the primary competition in the US Solana ETF market, though [Solana ETF inflows](https://www.coinspeaker.com/solana-sees-stablecoin-supply-surge-as-sol-etf-inflows-cross-150m/) reached $150 million across all products by Oct. 31.

The rapid progression from ETF to options contrasts with previous crypto ETF timelines. [Ethereum](https://www.coinspeaker.com/coins/ethereum/) [ETH$3 03724h volatility:0.6%Market cap:$366.52 BVol. 24h:$17.26 B](https://www.coinspeaker.com/coins/ethereum/)
ETF options launched on Oct. 23, 2025, approximately 15 months after the spot ETF debut in July 2024.

## Market Activity

The Solana ecosystem has experienced increased activity alongside institutional product launches. [Solana DEX volumes](https://www.coinspeaker.com/solana-outperforms-rivals-as-dex-volumes-surpass-5b/) surpassed $5 billion in early November, with network metrics showing sustained growth in transaction volume and user activity.

Institutional adoption of Solan

sources.title: Bitwise Asset Management launches the first US spot ETF for Solana

[Click here to see all results for](https://www.insidermonkey.com/search/) [Sorry, no results has been found matching your query.](javascript:void(0))

### Share

On October 28, 2025, Bitwise Asset Management launched the BSOL and therefore joined the crypto ETF club.  It’s marketed as the first US ETF to grant spot access to Solana and also offer staking rewards.  This shows how complex the crypto ETF market has become.

The product will likely capture investors’ interest, as it offers many of the features they are looking for.  It’s a way to diversify from Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, and the dual offering of price exposure and staking yield is an interesting innovation.

## What are Crypto ETFs?

Crypto ETFs are exchange-traded funds whose value is tied to crypto.  That way, it gives investors a chance to tie their investments to crypto prices without [using crypto](https://www.webopedia.com/crypto/exchanges/) exchanges or buying crypto coins themselves.

The ETFs have quickly become a staple of investment portfolios, as they provide a good balance to hedge against more risk-averse investments.  New altcoins are joining in quick succession, and it’s widely seen as a way to bridge the gap between crypto and traditional investments.

## The Road to BSOL: New SEC Rules and a Fast-Track Path

Until just recently, the process for getting an ETF spot approved was long and complicated, and it took Bitcoin over a year.  However, in September this year, the SEC approved a generic list of standard cryptos eligible for the spot.

This regulatory shift made things easier for Bitwise, and it’s part of a broader pro-crypto attitude the current administration has taken.  There are even talks of setting up stablecoin ETFs, though these are of lesser value to investors.  Cryptos are already widely adopted, and now that a regulatory landscape has made them safe, the government has taken a pro-business approach.

## How the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF Works

The design centers on two pillars:

**Spot SOL exposure**.  The fund holds all of its assets in Solana tokens.  This means the fund’s value depends on the market price of Solana.  In this regard, the fund is similar to any other crypto ETF.

**Staking reward capture**.  The fund aims to stake 100 percent of its SOL holdings.  That way, holders also receive rewards from staking as if they owned actual Solana coins.  These rewards are then added back to the fund.  It’s the first ETF to do so.

A Blockbuster Debut: Volume, Flows, and Industry Reaction

The launch made somewhat of a splash within the industry.  Millions of dollars in inflows came in as soon as the ETF was listed, and strong trading activity indicated market appetite for such a financial product.

The launch also caused a scramble among other issuers such as Grayscale Investments and VanEck Associates Corporation.  They were in the process of launching their own Solana products, and the Bitwise success caused them to hurry along.  According to experts, the ETF market has been rising ever since the product was first introduced.

Why Solana

Solana offers a compelling alternative to Bitcoin.  It has a high-throughput, low-fee layer-1 blockchain with an active DeFi ecosystem and supports tokenization and NFTs.  The large, liquid futures also made it a great subject for the ETF spot, making the authorization process easier.

The second wave of crypto ETFs includes smaller altcoins after Bitcoin and Ethereum have proven profitable.  Solana is therefore the perfect candidate to lead this wave.  The additional staking feature is also a plus, since it shows how innovative cryptos can be within traditional finance.

## The Risks

There are also risk

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a high-tech institutional vault as the 'Semantic Anchor' because the story is about a secure, institutional-grade financial product (the BSOL ETF) holding a digital asset. The vault represents the security and scale of the 'institutional' world. The core narrative of demand overwhelming supply is visualized by a massive torrent of liquid light (representing $260T of capital) being funneled into the vault, which holds the finite Solana. For the homepage, I integrated the ticker 'BSOL' diegetically, laser-etched onto the vault door to directly tie the concept to the specific product.
visual_idea.description: A full-height shot of a colossal, sleek institutional vault door made of polished obsidian and brushed titanium. A massive, brilliant torrent of golden liquid light, representing institutional capital, is being funneled down into a small opening at the top of the vault, creating immense pressure and a bright, hazy atmosphere. The ticker 'BSOL' is cleanly laser-etched into the titanium frame of the door. The background is a vast, dark, cavernous space, suggesting the depth of the global financial market, with subtle, out-of-focus architectural elements.

The door is expertly crafted from deeply polished obsidian and refined brushed titanium, exuding an institutional futurist aesthetic. A massive, brilliant torrent of golden liquid light, symbolizing institutional capital, powerfully funnels downwards into a small opening at the very top of the vault. This creates immense pressure and bathes the scene in a bright, hazy atmosphere, leveraging cinematic volumetric lighting. The ticker 'BSOL' is cleanly laser-etched directly into the brushed titanium frame of the door, subtly reflecting the golden light. The background recedes into a vast, dark, cavernous space, profoundly suggesting the boundless depth of the global financial market, with subtle, out-of-focus architectural elements barely visible in the gloom. The composition emphasizes extreme height and epic scale, with significant negative space above the vault door, making the subject feel monumental. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, cinematic lighting, volumetric lighting, high dynamic range, photorealistic, dramatic depth of field.

This creates a tense, hyperrealistic, and institutional futurist aesthetic, with an extremely shallow depth-of-field, global illumination, and ambient occlusion in the material creases. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, physically based rendering (PBR), high dynamic range, minimalist luxury, studio macro lens, zero distortion.

editorial.headline: Bitwise CIO Confirms ETF Demand Now Triples Daily Solana Issuance
editorial.standfirst: Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matthew Hougan reveals that institutional ETF inflows are currently absorbing 3x to 4x the daily new supply of SOL. This structural imbalance signals a looming supply shock as the $260 trillion global investment market begins its rotation into Solana.
key_takeaways: Since its October 28 launch, the Bitwise Solana ETF (BSOL) has consistently purchased 3x to 4x the daily network issuance of SOL.
Institutional inflows are averaging $40 million to $50 million per day, with zero days of net outflows recorded in the fund's history.
Financial advisors control access to a $260 trillion market but spend only three minutes per week analyzing crypto allocations, making the ETF wrapper the sole viable entry point.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The most critical signal for institutional investors is not sentiment, but the raw mechanics of supply and demand. **Bitwise** CIO Matthew Hougan presented data confirming that the Solana market is currently undergoing a severe supply squeeze. Since the launch of **BSOL** on October 28, the fund has been purchasing SOL at a rate of three to four times the network's daily production. Unlike the Bitcoin ETF launch, which absorbed roughly 2x the daily supply, the Solana ETF market is demonstrating a more aggressive absorption rate relative to issuance. This creates a programmatic upward pressure on price as **BSOL** systematically removes liquid SOL from circulation to be cold-stored and staked.

The "Alpha" here lies in the distribution channel rather than the technology. Hougan highlighted a stark reality: the $260 trillion global equity and fixed income market views the entire $3.1 trillion crypto industry as a rounding error. However, 70% of these institutions refuse to allocate capital without an ETF wrapper. With financial advisors spending an average of just three minutes per week analyzing crypto markets, the ETF is not merely a convenience—it is the prerequisite bridge for sovereign wealth funds and family offices. The current inflows of $40 million to $50 million per day represent only the initial friction being removed for this massive capital pool.

Looking beyond immediate flows, the trajectory of Gold ETFs suggests this is the beginning of a multi-year accumulation cycle. Hougan frames Solana as a "double bet": a wager on the exponential growth of tokenization and stablecoins—sectors the SEC Chair suggested could expand 100,000x—combined with Solana capturing dominant market share. As **BSOL** integrates staking rewards, a feature distinct from Bitcoin products, institutional capital is effectively capturing the network's economic yield. If the Gold ETF historical fractal holds true, these inflows will accelerate annually, compounding the scarcity effect over a decade-long horizon.
